task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly implements the trimmed mean algorithm, handles some but not all edge cases.
trace: To write the trimmed_mean function, I first handle empty and None inputs by returning None. Then, I sort the list to find both the minimum and maximum values easily. The median value is calculated as the middle element of the sorted list. I then filter out one occurrence of the minimum and maximum values from the list using a list comprehension. Finally, the average (mean) of the remaining numbers is calculated and returned as a float. An edge case considered here is when the list has an odd number of elements, where the median is the middle element instead of the average of the two middle elements.
